mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:2008-2009 rate cuts compressed equity risk premiums
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9.0 percent, it signals a confirmed bottom in the labor market, triggering institutional buying of cyclical equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
evidence_refs: fact:historical equity rallies accelerate after unemployment peaks
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies earnings revision momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:co-movement of housing and retail spending predicts equity outperformance
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns against HOUST and RSAFS growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recoveries, a rising industrial production index reflects capacity utilization rebounding from trough levels, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output leads earnings revisions in early recovery phases
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns during identified early-cycle windows, or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5 percent triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:inflation surprises historically cause short-term equity drawdowns
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 60% of CPI surprise events exceeding 0.5 percent, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting positive equity returns over a six-month investment horizon regardless of short-term volatility.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth correlates positively with trailing twelve-month earnings revisions
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across all available data, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in nonfarm payrolls and decline in the unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, which reduces wage-driven cost pressures while boosting consumer spending, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:coherent labor market signals precede equity outperformance
failure_condition: The cross-product of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to explain incremental variance in 20-day forward returns beyond their individual effects, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in housing starts exceeding 550 thousand units signals immediate construction sector momentum and consumer confidence, triggering short-term algorithmic buying that pushes S&P 500 prices higher within one week.
evidence_refs: fact:housing data releases historically cause short-term equity volatility and directional moves
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST crosses 550k, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC in a 2-year walk-forward test.
